failureDiagnosis.description: No deal was possible: supplier's minimum ($190.00) exceeds manufacturer's maximum ($180.00). The price gap of $10.00 makes any deal impossible.

why: supplier_manufacturer: supplier walked away in round 7 — no ZOPA: seller min ($190.00) > buyer max ($180.00) by $10.00; last offer was $200.00
manufacturer_retailer: deal at $260.00 in 6 rounds — ZOPA was $10.00 (seller min $250.00 → buyer max $260.00); seller margin: +$10.00, buyer margin: +$0.00
issues: [DEADLOCK SIGNAL] manufacturer rejected 3 times without making a counter-offer in supplier_manufacturer.
[NEGATIVE MARGIN] manufacturer offered $300.00 in round 1 of manufacturer_retailer — margin was -50.00 (below reservation price $250.00). Agent accepted/proposed a loss-making deal.
[NEGATIVE MARGIN] manufacturer offered $275.00 in round 3 of manufacturer_retailer — margin was -25.00 (below reservation price $250.00). Agent accepted/proposed a loss-making deal.
[NEGATIVE MARGIN] manufacturer offered $260.00 in round 5 of manufacturer_retailer — margin was -10.00 (below reservation price $250.00). Agent accepted/proposed a loss-making deal.
[STALE DATA] 1 agent(s) received stale market prices in manufacturer_retailer, potentially anchoring negotiations incorrectly.
what_to_fix: manufacturer should counter-offer instead of repeatedly rejecting — pure rejection leads to deadlock.
Constrain manufacturer: do not offer or accept prices that violate your reservation price (margin < 0).
